_meta.title: A2UI message-lifecycle decision layer (TKT-0016) — BUILD plan: GRAMMAR insertion, a2ui-compose servicing, corpus exemplar, a2ui-live transcript extension. Design intake output only; build dispatched separately per ADR-0126.
_meta.author: design intake (no component-design skill — teaching+demo shape)
_meta.grounding: SPEC a2ui-message-lifecycle.spec.md SPEC-R1..R5 under ADR-0126 (proposed, F2/F5 await Kim). Mechanics verified directly: renderer/tree.ts:85 (updateComponents whole-record upsert), renderer/renderer.ts:283-286 (deleteSurface DOM+store teardown), system-prompt.ts's OUTPUT_MARKER slice mechanics (ADR-0089/0090/0091), transcript.ts's shipped turn 1/2 script, a2ui-training-corpus.spec.md SPEC-R2/ADR-0064 (single-surface exemplar rule already covers deleteSurface).
_meta.note: OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the four surfaces a build slice touches (prompt/skill/corpus/site). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the shipped teaching layer must DO once built. plan:true — no build source exists yet; this decomp is dispatched to the appropriate build seats AFTER Kim rules on ADR-0126 F2/F5.

nodes.label: four-type choice bullets + deleteSurface wire shape appended in the OUTPUT_MARKER-onward zone (LLD-C1)
nodes.accept: system-prompt.ts's GRAMMAR literal contains the new bullets after the updateDataModel bullet and before the action-report bullet; assertMarkersHold() still passes at module load (neither marker string moved); buildSystemPrompt(catalog, []) contains 'deleteSurface' (SPEC-R1)

nodes.label: system-prompt-grammar.test.ts additions — teaching present + survives specific/blue-sky mode composition (LLD-C2)
nodes.accept: npm test system-prompt-grammar — new cases green: prompt contains 'deleteSurface'/'updateDataModel alone'/'FRESH surfaceId'/'REPLACES its ENTIRE record'; the mode-composition case passes for BOTH 'specific' and 'blue-sky' with zero grammarFor edits (SPEC-R1, R2)

nodes.label: prompt-drift.test.ts re-run clean (no catalog-inventory disturbance)
nodes.accept: npm test prompt-drift — the existing SET-EQUAL component/function inventory assertions stay green unchanged (additive GRAMMAR prose only, no catalog-section edit)

nodes.label: mental model widened three->four kinds + deleteSurface entry (LLD-C3)
nodes.accept: SKILL.md's Mental model section lists FOUR message kinds including deleteSurface with its shape and a citation to a2ui-message-lifecycle.spec.md SPEC-R1 rule 4

nodes.label: new Common-trap entry — whole-record upsert (LLD-C3)
nodes.accept: SKILL.md's Common traps list carries a new entry stating a resent id REPLACES the whole node record, citing renderer/tree.ts's components.set(comp.id, comp) and SPEC-R2

nodes.label: References table gains the new SPEC row
nodes.accept: SKILL.md's References table lists a2ui-message-lifecycle.spec.md with a 'use when' line naming the message-type decision rule

nodes.label: worked kpi-panel seed authored — single surfaceId, all four types in order
nodes.accept: a new exemplar seed exists under src/examples/ matching the LLD-C4 worked shape (createSurface/updateComponents x2/updateDataModel x2/deleteSurface, one surfaceId throughout)

nodes.label: fixture test — validates 0 errors at every stream prefix, one surfaceId, SPEC-R2-compliant resends
nodes.accept: a new fixture test (the examples.test.ts precedent) asserts the shared validator returns 0 errors at each prefix of the seed's a2uiOutput and that the root's resend in the restructure step carries its FULL prior prop set (SPEC-R4 AC1/AC2)

nodes.label: corpus admission (import/dedup/judge)
nodes.justify: out-of-scope — explicitly NOT owed by this intake, rides the standing corpus-gap follow-up (token-surfaces M2 wave); listed for trace completeness only

nodes.label: turns 3-5 authored in transcript.ts, continuing the shipped canvas/confirmation script
nodes.accept: transcript.ts's recordedTranscript.turns grows from 2 to 5 entries; turn 3 restructures the SAME canvas surfaceId (whole-record root resend per SPEC-R2), turn 4 carries EXACTLY one updateDataModel line and no updateComponents/createSurface, turn 5 carries exactly one deleteSurface targeting 'confirmation'; each carries a note stating which type fired and why

nodes.label: round-trip.test.ts extension — validity + data-only-turn assertion + post-delete DOM check
nodes.accept: npm test round-trip — new cases green: turns 3-5 validate 0 errors at each prefix; turn 4's lines.length === 1 and its parsed message key is 'updateDataModel'; after ingesting all 5 turns through a fresh createRenderer + mount, no confirmation-surface DOM node remains while a canvas-rooted node still exists (SPEC-R5 AC1, AC2, AC4)

nodes.label: a2ui-live.ts manual verification pass (no code change expected)
nodes.accept: a manual npm run dev walk of a2ui-live confirms the JSON/HTML tabs and the note-bearing chat bubble already surface each of turns 3-5 correctly with NO page code change; any gap found is escalated as an LLD repair, not patched ad hoc (SPEC-R5 AC3)

nodes.label: ADR-0126 ratification gate
nodes.accept: Kim has ruled on F2 (GRAMMAR insertion mechanism) and F5 (delete-vs-keep heuristic) and ADR-0126's status is accepted BEFORE n2/n14 are built — a build against a still-proposed ADR is out of sequence

actions.label: teach the four-type choice + deleteSurface's wire shape to the live-agent machine prompt, in every mode
prove the teaching survives mode composition without new grammarFor plumbing
prove the insertion does not disturb the catalog-derived inventory gate
widen the human-facing a2ui-compose mental model to all four message kinds
warn composers that a resent component id replaces its whole record
point composers at the new SPEC as the decision-rule authority
author one worked, single-surface, all-four-type corpus exemplar
prove the exemplar validates cleanly at every stream prefix and respects the whole-record-resend rule
leave admission to its own standing follow-up, not this build
extend the shipped a2ui-live script to complete the four-type arc on the existing surfaces
prove the extension's validity, the data-only turn's message purity, and the post-delete DOM state
verify the existing demo chrome already surfaces the new turns with no code change
gate the whole build behind Kim's ADR-0126 ruling

edges.why: ADR-0126 F2 rules exactly what the insertion mechanism is — building it ahead of ratification risks a wasted rewrite
ADR-0126 F5 rules the delete-vs-keep specifics turn 5 depends on
the test asserts the insertion this node makes
the drift gate re-run proves the insertion didn't touch the catalog-derived section
both land in the same SKILL.md mental-model/traps edit
the References row points at the SPEC the trap entry cites
the fixture test runs over the authored seed
admission is the next stage after a green fixture test, explicitly deferred
the exemplar's deleteSurface/updateComponents shape must match the SAME rule the prompt now teaches (single source of truth, SPEC-R3)
the test proves the authored turns
the manual demo walk is only meaningful once the underlying transcript is proven correct
the demo's note-annotation mechanism carries the SAME per-turn rationale the prompt teaching now names explicitly
